channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Archaeological Discoveries in Mara Reveal Ancient East African Connections and Growing Threats to Heritage Sites
item.description: New archaeological research in Tanzania’s Mara Region is revealing important evidence that ancient communities around Lake Victoria were more connected than previously thought. Excavations in areas su...

item.title: How alcohol affects sleep quality and why it is not a reliable sleep aid
item.description: This article by Dr. Rébecca Robillard examines the complex relationship between alcohol consumption and sleep quality. While many people believe alcohol helps them fall asleep more easily, the science...

item.title: Angeles City hotel guest recalls waking up to nearby building collapse; at least four dead
item.description: A devastating building collapse in Angeles City, Pampanga has left at least four people dead, 17 missing, and 26 others rescued, according to authorities as search and rescue operations continue. The ...

item.title: Environmental coalition proposes expanding local water supply in Southern California to 85% by 2045, opposing Delta tunnel project
item.description: A coalition of environmental and conservation groups is urging Southern California to dramatically increase its reliance on locally sourced water, arguing that the region could meet up to 85% of its w...

item.title: PDP delegation condoles Governor Makinde over Oyo school abductions and killings
item.description: Leaders of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) have paid a condolence visit to the Governor of Oyo State, Seyi Makinde, following a disturbing kidnapping incident that affected schools in the state. Th...

item.title: Tooth to host intimate release party for debut EP ‘Restless In Bloom’ at Soho’s The Blue Posts
item.description: London rock band Tooth are preparing to celebrate the release of their debut EP ‘Restless In Bloom’ with a series of intimate events, including a special release party at Soho pub The Blue Posts on 12...

item.title: Dye & Durham appoints Mary Filippelli as board chair and adds Tyler Proud to board
item.description: Dye & Durham Ltd. announced several leadership and governance changes, including the appointment of Mary Filippelli as chair of the company’s board of directors until the next annual meeting. Filippel...

item.title: Australia’s first astronaut invited to consider International Space Station mission during Western Australia visit
item.description: Katherine Bennell-Pegg, recognised as Australia’s first qualified astronaut under the national flag, has revealed she has been invited by the European Space Agency (ESA) to participate in a potential ...

item.title: IMD warns of severe heatwave in Vidarbha while parts of Maharashtra prepare for light rains and thunderstorms
item.description: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for the Vidarbha region of Maharashtra, including districts such as Amravati, Wardha, and Chandrapur, where extreme temperatures a...

item.title: Trump Pressures Middle Eastern Nations to Join Abraham Accords as Part of Iran Deal
item.description: Former President Donald Trump announced a controversial diplomatic demand directed at several Muslim-majority countries in the Middle East and South Asia, including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Turk...

item.title: Jayden Adams says domestic success of Sundowns and Pirates can boost Bafana Bafana ahead of World Cup
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams believes the recent success of both Sundowns and Orlando Pirates will have a positive impact on Bafana Bafana as they prepare for the upcoming Fifa World Cup ...

item.title: Recto and several officials face plunder complaint over alleged PhilHealth fund transfer issues

item.title: Galeries Tower secures first overall pick in PVL Rookie Draft
item.description: Galeries Tower will have the privilege of selecting first overall in the upcoming Premier Volleyball League (PVL) Rookie Draft, as reported in a sports update by ABS-CBN News. This development positio...

item.title: Robert Reich reflects on Memorial Day, honoring a fallen Vietnam War friend and contrasting duty with political rhetoric about military service
item.description: In this Memorial Day opinion essay, former U.S. Labor Secretary Robert Reich reflects on the meaning of service, sacrifice, and civic duty through the memory of his college friend, Robbie, who was kil...

item.title: Mbadi cautions leaders against misinformation on Finance Bill 2026
item.description: National Treasury Cabinet Secretary John Mbadi has called on political leaders in Kenya to avoid spreading propaganda and misleading information regarding the Finance Bill 2026. Speaking in Nairobi, M...

item.title: JioStar executive warns cricket rights may be overvalued as buyer competition shrinks in India
item.description: Uday Shankar, vice-chair of India’s JioStar, has warned that global cricket boards risk overpricing media rights and weakening the sport’s most important revenue market. Speaking about the state of cr...

item.title: Texas GOP Senate Runoff: Ken Paxton Leads John Cornyn in Costly Primary Battle
item.description: In a fiercely contested Republican primary runoff for the U.S. Senate seat in Texas, Attorney General Ken Paxton, who has received endorsement from former President Donald Trump, appears to hold a lea...

item.title: Founder of viral Indian Gen Z account says platform faced crackdown and intimidation
item.description: A viral social media movement in India focusing on Gen Z concerns has come under scrutiny after its founder alleged that the platform was targeted through a crackdown, hacking attempts, and threats di...

item.title: Former SNP chief executive Peter Murrell jailed after admitting £400,000 embezzlement
item.description: Peter Murrell, the estranged husband of former Scottish First Minister Nicola Sturgeon and long-serving chief executive of the Scottish National Party (SNP), has been remanded in custody after pleadin...

item.title: 1938 Richmond tobacco workers protest over wages and housing inequality
item.description: This historical photograph captures a moment of labor protest in Richmond, Virginia, during October 1938, in the midst of the Great Depression. The image shows an unidentified woman standing on a stre...
